interactions.summary: Sneako and Jiang set up the call immediately after Sneako reports being attacked; Sneako says reports about a knockout or tooth loss are exaggerated.
interactions.speaker_attribution: SPEAKER_02 appears to be Sneako; SPEAKER_00 appears to be Jiang.

interactions.summary: Jiang says the political temperature in the United States is high enough that his wife worries he could be attacked or refused entry if he travels there.
Sneako frames the attack as possibly organized and uses it to justify panels where ideological opponents can disagree civilly.
Jiang says he has real disagreements with Dugin but wants honest conversations with people from different political beliefs.

speaker_notes.note: The early speaker mapping is stable: SPEAKER_00 is Jiang and SPEAKER_02 is Sneako.

claims.claim: Jiang treats the United States in April 2026 as politically volatile enough that public travel or border entry could carry personal risk for him.
Jiang presents cross-ideological conversation as necessary even when he strongly disagrees with another participant.

signature_moments.moment: Jiang imagines being recognized in New York as the person who will start a war and then being attacked.
signature_moments.source_phrase: you're the ass who will start this war
signature_moments.why_it_matters: The interview opens with the stakes of prediction becoming personal exposure, not abstract commentary.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: A measure of public volatility high enough to affect travel, speech, and personal safety.

chronology_notes.note: By 2026-04-15 Jiang says he is hesitant to do a U.S. podcast circuit because the domestic temperature keeps rising.
